title: Reconcile eu7 AC-075 recall assert to a one-sided CI gate (floor constant unchanged)
status: COMPLETE — committed, NOT merged/pushed (gated; orchestrator runs codex §9)
hitl_ruling: 2026-06-08 — 0.8960 ACCEPTED for 0.8.0 (0.90 floor inside the 95% CI); reconcile gate WITHOUT lowering the floor

assert_change.before: assert!(*recall >= CURRENT_FLOOR, ...) — point-estimate hard-assert; PANICKED (exit 101) at the measured 0.896
assert_change.threading: verdict tuple widened (usize,bool,bool,f64,bool) -> (usize,bool,bool,f64,f64,bool) to carry rec.ci_hi (recall_ci_hi) from the same RecallResult; both verdict loops updated
assert_change.json_record: ac_013b record now emits passes_current_floor_point_estimate AND passes_ci_gate_0_8_0_one_sided; point estimate + ci_lo/ci_hi/sigma still reported

gate_predicate.fn: recall_ci_clears_floor(ci_hi: f64, floor: f64) -> bool { ci_hi >= floor }
gate_predicate.semantics: PASS iff recall 95% bootstrap CI is NOT significantly below the floor (one-sided). Deliberately NOT a two-sided floor-within-[ci_lo,ci_hi] test.
gate_predicate.shared: included via #[path] in eu7_real_corpus_ac.rs and ga2_vector_stage_seam.rs

floor_constants_unchanged.confirmation: no constant lowered; assert restored to a statistically-honest CI form, not weakened/removed

cases.passes_recorded_real: mean=0.896 (< floor, guarded), ci_hi=0.925 >= 0.90 => gate PASSES
cases.not_two_sided: ci_hi=0.957 (high recall, ci_lo>floor) PASSES — a two-sided test would wrongly fail it

focused_test.run: cargo test -p fathomdb-engine --test ga2_vector_stage_seam => 2 passed; 0 failed

compile.eu7_real_corpus_ac: ok (--no-run under --features 'default-embedder operator'); not executed — real run is ~2.5h and infeasible/unnecessary here

next_eu7_run: The recorded perf-canonical eu7 result (N=7667 ci_hi 0.925) now satisfies the reconciled gate => the next eu7 run exits 0 (no panic). Source: dev/plans/runs/GA-signoff-eu7-remeasure-20260608T172804Z.json.
doc_amendments: dev/acceptance.md AC-075: added the 0.8.0 GA-3 asserting form (recall_ci_hi >= 0.90, one-sided), floor target 0.90 unchanged, cited measured point 0.896 / CI [0.864,0.925] => PASS, flagged point-estimate recovery + ~4pt drop diagnosis as 0.8.1, marked CI-form as 0.8.0-scoped reconciliation to be revisited after 0.8.0
dev/adr/ADR-0.7.0-vector-binary-quant.md §2 point 4: short amendment recording the one-sided CI gate (constant 0.90 unchanged), citing GA-signoff result + 0.8.1 recovery/revisit
eu7 module header recall-posture note + tier-2 verdict comment updated to the CI gate
secondary_done: eu7 JSON output repointed from dev/plans/runs/0.7.1-EU-7-measurements.json to dev/plans/runs/eu7-latest-measurements.json (one line) — stops clobbering the historical 0.937 anchor; the legacy anchor file is left intact
deviations: Factored the gate predicate into a shared #[path] support module (tests/support/recall_gate.rs) so the SAME predicate the eu7 assert reads is the one unit-tested — avoids duplicating the gate logic in the focused test. Within the prompt's 'factor the gate predicate so it's unit-testable' instruction.

Renamed the JSON field passes_current_floor -> passes_current_floor_point_estimate and added passes_ci_gate_0_8_0_one_sided, since the asserting verdict is no longer the point estimate. Report-only data; no consumer code depends on the old field (only a comment string in eu8_ir_validation.rs references the legacy filename, unaffected).
